rationale.root_cause: At the FLAW site in bad(), memcpy uses sizeof(*structCharVoid) (the whole struct) to copy into charFirst, overflowing past the 16-byte array into voidSecond/voidThird and corrupting the pointer. This concrete overflow causes printLine to dereference an ASCII-filled pointer (e.g., 0x3736353433323130), leading to a crash.
rationale.local_fix: Change memcpy to use sizeof(structCharVoid->charFirst) as the copy size so only the 16-byte field is written. This prevents overwriting the adjacent pointer fields.
rationale.global_fix: Adopt a bounded field-copy helper or typed buffer wrapper (e.g., memcpy_s or a helper that deduces sizeof destination via an ARRAY_SIZE macro) so the destination field’s size is used and struct size cannot be confused with field size. This enforces correct bounds at the call site across the codebase.
